rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO and ANNA GEHRIGER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer as the motion to strike First Am. Complaint 76, 91, 92, 99, 101, 108, 111 and 112 and Prayer for Relief 1, 2 and 3. Granted, with 10 days leave to amend as to First Am. Complaint, 91, 92. Granted, without leave to amend, as to First Am. Complaint, 99. The allegations of the breach of fiduciary duty claim does not support punitive or exemplary damages. Exemplary damages are not recoverable for negligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO and ANNA GEHRIGER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the third, seventh and ninth causes of action. Overruled as the fifth and sixth causes of action. For the third cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts to allege breach of contract whether it be oral, written or implied in fact. (CACI no. 303. See First Am. Complaint, 12 - 16, 74 - 76.) For the fifth cause of action, the legal authority cited by Defendants, Buehler v. Shardellati (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 1125, 1143 - 1143 does not stand for the proposition asserted. (Points & Auth., 5:1-3.) The Buehler Court did not find that a breach of fiduciary duty claim must be dismissed as redundant if it based on the same facts as a negligence claim. For the sixth cause of action, Defendants demur only to the negligence per se allegations, and not the negligence allegations. A demurrer does not lie as to part of a cause of action. For the seventh cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts to allege extreme, and outrageous conduct. For the ninth cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts with the requisite specificity to allege fraud. The allegations of the third, sixth and ninth causes of action are not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. HARCOURT GROUP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN HANLIN's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Per order of the Presiding Judge, this motion shall be heard in Department 302. All future motions, except discovery motions handled by the pro tempore system, shall be calendared in Department 501 and shall be assigned by the Presiding Judge on a motion to motion basis. Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. Although Robert Pechoultres may verify the answer on behalf of all Cross-Defendant Owners, the verification is insufficient. (CCP § 446.) Mr. Pechoultres' verification is based only on his information and belief. (Cross-Defendant Owners' Verified Answer, p. 38:15-16.) Cross-Defendant Owners' request for monetary sanctions is denied. Further, CCP § 128.5 cannot form the basis for monetary sanctions in this action. (See CCP § 128.5(b)(1).) rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. HARCOURT GROUP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike The Verification And The Entirety Of The Verified Answer Of Sojourn Properties Inc. And X-Deft Steven G. King To The Verified X-Complt Of John Hanlin And To Enter Default To X-Deft Steven G. King Pursuant To Ccp 435, 436, 437 And 446; (Crc 3.1322)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN HANLIN's MOTION To Strike The Verification And The Entirety Of The Verified Answer Of Sojourn Properties Inc. And Cross-Defendant Steven G. King To The Verified Cross-Complt Of John Hanlin And To Enter Default To Cross-Deft Steven G. King Pursuant To Ccp 435, 436, 437 And 446. Per order of the Presiding Judge, this motion shall be heard in Department 302. All future motions, except discovery motions handled by the pro tempore system, shall be calendared in Department 501 and shall be assigned by the Presiding Judge on a motion to motion basis. Denied. The verification of Steven G. King is sufficient for verification individually and on behalf of Sojourn Properties. (CCP § 446.) Although not included as a Cross-Defendant in the title of the Hanlin Cross-Complaint, Mr. Hanlin references Sojourn Properties as a Cross-Defendant. (Hanlin Cross-Complaint, 20.) Given this uncertainty, it was appropriate for Sojourn Properties to file a verified answer to the Hanlin Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendants request for sanctions is denied. (CCP § 128.7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: VICTOR S. BRICE VS. MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. Cross-Defendant Victor Brice ("Brice") has not met his burden of making a prima facie showing that the Cross-Complaint arises out of an act in furtherance of his right to petition or free speech. (CCP §425.16.) Brice's memorandum of points and authorities does not engage in any meaningful analysis of how the Cross-Complaint arises from protected activity. To the extent that Brice argues the Cross-Complaint arises out of statements made about his representation in the Robinson v. Brice action, the determination of who was acting as his counsel does not "relate to the substantive issues in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) To the extent that Brice argues the Cross-Complaint arises from his anticipation of the Brice v. GuideOne action or the Brice v. Mastiastic, Roth, & Johnson action, claims that an attorney breached a fiduciary duty to a client, or otherwise violated ethical rules, are considered not to involve protected speech or petitioning activity. (Freeman v Schack (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 719, 732; see also U.S. Fire Ins. Co. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter, & Hampton (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1617.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR S. BRICE VS. MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. A motion to strike can be used to reach defects or objections to pleadings that are not challengeable by demurrer. (Pierson v. Sharp Memorial Hospital, Inc. (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 340, 342; see also Weil and Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:156.) A general demurrer lies if the litigation privilege bars allegations on the face of the complaint. (See e.g. Silberg v. Anderson (199) 50 Cal.3d 205, 211; see also Weil and Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:60.10.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR S. BRICE VS. MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR BRICE's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. A cause of action for negligent misrepresentation accrues when the complaining party learns, or was put on notice that a representation was false. (Brandon v. Gray (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 29, 35.) The Cross-Complaint alleges that the falsity of the representation was not learned until the filing of the underlying complaint. (Cross-Complaint, par. 28.) The Cross-Complaint is not uncertain. The Court did not consider the text of the letter set forth in Cross-Defendant's reply brief, as a demurrer is limited to the allegations on the face of the pleadings or matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwin (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAMIE S. BLANZA VS. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fir Insurance Company Of Pittsburh Pa And Lotsolutions, Inc Person (S) Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, May 07, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JAMIE BLANZA's Motion To Compel Depositions Of National Union Fir Insurance Company Of Pittsburh Pa And Lotsolutions, Inc Person (S) Most Knowledgeable Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ISABEL WISKIND VS. VILLA FLORENCE HOSPITALITY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, May 7, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANTs VILLA FLORENCE HOSPITALITY, INC., JRK HOTEL GROUP INC. and JAMES IRBY's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
